items.name: Martha's Table
items.access_script: Email info@marthastable.ca or visit 629 Princess Street, Kingston, ON K7L 1E2. No fees.

items.name: Memorial Centre Farmers Market
items.access_script: Visit https://www.memorialcentrefarmersmarket.ca for information.

items.name: Métis Nation of Ontario (Kingston Office)
items.access_script: Call 613-549-1674 or email contactus@metisnation.org. MNO Citizenship card (for some programs) required. No fees.

items.name: Métis Nation of Ontario Crisis Line
items.name_fr: Métis Nation of Ontario Crisis Line
items.access_script: Métis Nation of Ontario citizens can call 1-877-767-7572 for mental health and addictions support. This crisis line is free and operates 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: MMIWG Crisis Line
items.access_script: Individuals impacted by missing and murdered Indigenous women, girls, or 2SLGBTQQIA+ people can call 1-844-413-6649 for support. This national crisis line is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: MS Knowledge Network (MS Canada)
items.name_fr: Réseau de connaissances sur la SP (SP Canada)
items.access_script: Call 1-844-859-6789 (Monday–Friday, 8 AM to 8 PM Eastern) to connect with an MS Navigator for information and support related to multiple sclerosis. This service is free and available to anyone affected by MS.

items.name: Naseeha Mental Health
items.access_script: Muslim youth and others can call or text 1-866-627-3342 at any time for free, anonymous mental health support. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: National Eating Disorder Information Centre (NEDIC)
items.access_script: Call 1-866-633-4220 or email nedic@uhn.ca. No fees.

items.name: National Overdose Response Service (NORS)
items.access_script: If you plan to use drugs alone, you can call 1-888-688-6677 so an operator stays on the line to monitor your safety and respond in case of overdose. This service is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Nishnawbe-Aski Legal Services (NALSC)
items.access_script: Indigenous residents of Nishnawbe Aski Nation territory can call 1-800-465-5581 for legal aid services. (Services are free of charge, but financial eligibility criteria may apply for ongoing assistance.)

items.name: ODSP Kingston Office
items.access_script: Visit https://www.ontario.ca for information.

items.name: Office of the Worker Adviser (OWA)
items.access_script: Non-unionized workers who have been injured on the job or faced reprisals can call 1-800-435-8980 for free legal advice and representation. The Office of the Worker Adviser helps with WSIB claims and occupational health and safety reprisal cases at no cost.

items.name: One Roof Kingston Youth Hub
items.access_script: Hi, I'm 20 and having a hard time finding a job and a place to live. Can I drop by to see a worker?

items.name: Ongwanada
items.access_script: Hi, I need information about support services for my child with a developmental disability.

items.name: Ontario Caregiver Helpline
items.name_fr: Ligne d'assistance aux aidants naturels de l'Ontario
items.access_script: Ontario caregivers can call 1-833-416-2273 at any time for information, assessment, and referrals to local support services. This helpline is free of charge.

items.name: Ontario Legal Information Centre
items.access_script: Call 1-844-343-7462 to request a free 30-minute consultation with a lawyer through the Ontario Legal Information Centre. This service is available to any Ontario resident, regardless of income, and no documents are required to access it.

items.name: Ontario Poison Centre
items.access_script: Call 1-800-268-9017 or email poison.information@sickkids.ca. No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Ontario Problem Gambling Helpline
items.name_fr: Ligne ontarienne d'aide sur le jeu problématique
items.access_script: Call 1-888-230-3505. No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: ONTX - Ontario Online & Text Crisis Services
items.name_fr: ONTX - Ontario Online & Text Crisis Services
items.access_script: Text the word "SUPPORT" to 258258 to connect with Ontario’s distress text line (available daily from 2:00 PM to 2:00 AM). If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: OPP Frontenac Detachment
items.access_script: Visit https://www.opp.ca for information.

items.name: Parkinson Canada
items.access_script: Call 1-888-664-1974 (Monday–Friday, 9 AM to 4 PM Eastern) for information and support on Parkinson’s disease. You can also email support@parkinson.ca for assistance.

items.name: Partners in Mission Food Bank
items.access_script: Hi, I'd like to register for a food hamper. What documents do you need me to bring?

items.name: PFLAG Canada
items.access_script: Call 1-888-530-6777 or email inquiries@pflagcanada.ca. No fees.

items.name: Providence Care Hospital
items.access_script: Call 613-544-4900 for assistance, or visit https://providencecare.ca.

items.name: Queen's Legal Aid
items.access_script: Hi, I've been charged with an offence and I can't afford a lawyer. Can I speak to someone at the clinic?

items.name: Queen's Legal Aid
items.access_script: Call 613-533-2102 for assistance.

items.name: Resolve Counselling Services Canada
items.access_script: Hi, I'm looking for counselling but I don't have insurance. Can you tell me about your sliding scale options?

items.name: ReStart Employment Services
items.access_script: Hi, I need help finding a job. Do I need an appointment to see an employment counsellor?

items.name: ReStart Employment Services
items.access_script: Call 613-542-7373 for assistance, or visit https://restartnow.ca.

items.name: Rideau Heights Community Centre
items.access_script: Visit 85 Maccauley St, Kingston, ON K7K 2V8. Fees: Varies by program.

items.name: Rural Frontenac Community Services
items.access_script: Call 613-279-3151 for assistance, or visit https://rfcs.ca.

items.name: Ryandale Transitional Housing
items.access_script: Hi, I'm currently on the street and need a place to warm up. Are you open for drop-in?

items.name: Salvation Army Community & Family Services
items.name_fr: Armée du Salut Services communautaires et familiaux
items.access_script: Call 613-548-4411 or email KingstonCitadel.CFS@salvationarmy.ca. ID, Proof of income/address required. No fees.

items.name: Salvation Army Housing Resource Centre
items.name_fr: Centre de ressources en logement de l'Armée du Salut
items.access_script: Hi, I'm behind on my rent and worried about losing my apartment. Can I apply for the rent bank?

items.name: Salvation Army Rideau Heights Corps
items.access_script: Hi, I live in Rideau Heights and need food assistance. Can I book an appointment for a hamper?

items.name: Seniors Association Kingston Region
items.access_script: Hi, I'm interested in joining the Seniors Association. Can I come in for a tour?

items.name: Seniors Safety Line
items.access_script: Call 1-866-299-1011 for confidential support and referrals if you or a senior you know is experiencing abuse. The Seniors Safety Line is free and open 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Service Canada Centre
items.access_script: Visit https://www.canada.ca for information.

items.name: ServiceOntario
items.access_script: Visit https://www.ontario.ca for information.
